mission.title: kubevirt: Increase code coverage by 10%
mission.description: Increase code coverage by 10%. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kubevirt deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kubevirt version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
helm list -n kubevirt 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubevirt installation.

steps.title: Review kubevirt configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubevirt configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl get configmap -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubevirt
```
We are tracking code coverage using goverage (shown here https://coveralls.io/github/kubevirt/kubevirt).

This issue is about adding tests to increase the code coverage.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Increase code coverage by 10%
steps.description:
I fear that most are already excluded. The only ones included are the defaulter and deepcopy files. They are tested with some fuzzing and have almost 100% coverage (another question is if it is really necessary since k8s would probably detect immediately if their generators are broken but it was just easy to do).

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl get events -n kubevirt --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Increase code coverage by 10%" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: I fear that most are already excluded. The only ones included are the defaulter and deepcopy files. They are tested with some fuzzing and have almost 100% coverage (another question is if it is really necessary since k8s would probably detect immediately if their generators are broken but it was just easy to do).

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubevirt installed or the issue environment reproducible.
